nameMeanings.name: Overman
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Dutch: status name from Middle Dutch overman ‘judge, arbitrator, go-between’.
English (East Anglia): occupational name from Middle English overman ‘leader, ruler; supervisor, overseer’, perhaps also ‘judge, arbitrator’ (compare 1 above).

variants.name: Overland
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Scandinavian Borge, Erik, Jan Erik, Nordahl, Tryg.

Norwegian (mainly Øverland): habitational name from any of some twenty farmsteads, mainly in Telemark and on the west coast, named Øverland, Overland, from øver, over ‘upper’ + land ‘(piece of) land, farmstead'.

English (Norfolk and Cambridgeshire): apparently a habitational name from Overland Farm in Ash next Sandwich (Kent), although the name is now strongly associated with northern East Anglia. The placename derives from Old English ōfer ‘bank’ + land ‘land’.

variants.name: Overmann
meanings: topographic name for someone who lived on the bank of a river, from Middle Low German over ‘river bank’ + man ‘man’.
status name for a judge or arbitrator (compare Dutch   Overman  ).

nameMeanings.name: Charles
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: From a Germanic word, karl, meaning ‘free man’, akin to Old English ceorl ‘man’. The name, Latin form Carolus, owed its popularity in medieval Europe to the Frankish leader Charlemagne ( ?742–814 ), who in 800 established himself as Holy Roman Emperor. His name (Latin Carolus Magnus) means ‘Charles the Great’. Carolus—or Karl, the German form—was a common name among Frankish leaders, including Charlemagne's grandfather Charles Martel ( 688–741 ). Charles is the French form. The name occurs occasionally in medieval Britain as Karolus or Carolus; it had a certain vogue in West Yorkshire from the 1400s, particularly among gentry families. The form Charles was chosen by Mary Queen of Scots ( 1542–87 ), who had been brought up in France, for her son, Charles James ( 1566–1625 ), who became King James VI of Scotland and, from 1603 , James I of England. His son and grandson both reigned as King Charles , and the name thus became established in the 17th century both in the Stuart royal house and among English and Scottish supporters of the Stuart monarchy. In the 18th century it was to some extent favoured, along with James , by Jacobites, supporters of the exiled Stuarts, opposed to the Hanoverian monarchy, especially in the Highlands of Scotland. In the 19th century the popularity of the name was further enhanced by romanticization of the story of ‘Bonnie Prince Charlie’, leader of the 1745 rebellion.

historicEvents.description: Thomas Jefferson's American Declaration of Independence endorsed by Congress. Colonies declare independence.

historicEvents.title: The First Constitution
historicEvents.description: Serving the newly created United States of America as the first constitution, the Articles of Confederation were an agreement among the 13 original states preserving the independence and sovereignty of the states. But with a limited central government, the Constitutional Convention came together to replace the Articles of Confederation with a more established Constitution and central government on where the states can be represented and voice their concerns and comments to build up the nation.

historicEvents.title: Becomes 12th State
historicEvents.description: On November 21, 1789, North Carolina became the 12th state in the Union.
Historical Boundaries: 1801: Clark, Indiana Territory, United States 1816: Clark, Indiana, United States
France sells Louisiana territories to U.S.A.

historicEvents.title: Financial Relief for Public Land
historicEvents.description: A United States law to provide financial relief for the purchasers of Public Lands. It permitted the earlier buyers, that couldn't pay completely for the land, to return the land back to the government. This granted them a credit towards the debt they had on land. Congress, also, extended credit to buyer for eight more years. Still while being in economic panic and the shortage of currency made by citizens, the government hoped that with the time extension, the economy would improve.

historicEvents.title: Trail of Tears
historicEvents.description: In the 1830's, President Jackson called for all the Native Americans to be forced off their own land. As the Cherokee were forced out of North Carolina many of them hid in the mountains of North Carolina.
historicEvents.urlTitle: North Carolina Trail of Tears Association

en.lifeSummary: Charles Overman was born in 1760, in North Carolina, United States. He married Mary Ann Scarborough in 1779, in North Carolina, United States. They were the parents of at least 3 sons and 5 daughters. He died on 29 April 1839, in Clark, Indiana, United States, at the age of 79.

fr.lifeSummary: Charles Overman est né en 1760, à Caroline du Nord, États-Unis. Il a épousé Mary Ann Scarborough en 1779, à Caroline du Nord, États-Unis. Ils ont eu au moins 3 fils et 5 filles. Il est décédé le 29 avril 1839, à Clark, Indiana, États-Unis, à l’âge de 79 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Charles Overman wurde 1760 in North Carolina, Vereinigte Staaten geboren. Er hat 1779 in North Carolina, Vereinigte Staaten Mary Ann Scarborough geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 3 Söhne und 5 Töchter. Er starb am 29. April 1839 im Alter von 79 Jahren in Clark, Indiana, Vereinigte Staaten.

ko.lifeSummary: Charles Overman(은)는 1760년 미국 노스캐롤라이나 주에서 태어났다. 그는 1779년 미국 노스캐롤라이나 주에서 Mary Ann Scarborough(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 세 명의 아들과 다섯 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1839년 4월 29일 미국 인디애나 주 클라르크에서 79세의 나이로 사망했다.
